steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best emphasizes a difference between the two lighthouse keepers. That means the correct answer must explicitly contrast Emery and Bennett — not just describe one, and not highlight a similarity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Margaret Emery and Clara Bennett both played crucial roles in ensuring safe navigation for watercraft in past centuries.: emphasizes what the keepers had in common rather than a difference. ✗
  • Choice B — As the lighthouse keeper at Point Fermin Light, Clara Bennett helped watercraft navigate at night.: describes only one keeper, so no contrast is drawn between them. ✗
  • Choice C — Margaret Emery worked as a lighthouse keeper in an earlier century than did Clara Bennett.: directly compares the two keepers — Emery served in an earlier century than Bennett, an explicit contrast supported by the notes (Emery: 1844–1862; Bennett: 1925–1941). ✓
  • Choice D — From 1844 to 1862, the nighttime waters of Maryland were made more navigable thanks to Margaret Emery.: describes only one keeper, so no contrast is drawn between them. ✗
C is correct.
